provenance.source: Twilight Imperium 4E technology cards, transcribed via the TI4 Fandom wiki
provenance.wiki_license: Fandom content is CC-BY-SA 3.0; attribution retained here.
provenance.ip_note: Mechanical card text is Fantasy Flight Games / Asmodee IP, vendored for a free fan tool (RAG grounding / citations), same basis as the LRR corpus.
provenance.unofficial_note: Discordant Stars is a fan-made expansion, not FFG-published; its entries carry set='discordant_stars'.

technologies.name: Applied Biothermics
technologies.text: When you pass, you may gain 1 commodity for each other player who has passed. ACTION: Exhaust this card.

technologies.name: Bio-Stims
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card at the end of your turn to ready 1 of your planets that has a technology specialty or 1 of your other technologies

technologies.name: Bioplasmosis
technologies.text: At the end of the status phase, you may remove any number of infantry from planets you control and place them on 1 or more planets you control in the same or adjacent systems

technologies.name: Blackmail Programs
technologies.text: When a player would perform a strategic action, you may exhaust this card and spend 1 token from your strategy pool to end that player's turn; the strategic action is not resolved and the strategy card is not exhausted.

technologies.name: Broker Network
technologies.text: Each planet you control that has a technology specialty or an attachment gains the PRODUCTION 1 ability as if it were a unit.

technologies.name: Dacxive Animators
technologies.text: After you win a ground combat, you may place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on that planet

technologies.name: Envoy Network
technologies.text: At the start of the agenda phase, you may choose and exhaust 1 cultural, 1 hazardous, and 1 industrial planet. When you cast 1 or more votes, if you exhaust at least 1 cultural, 1 hazardous, and 1 industrial planet to cast votes, you may cast 4 additional votes.

technologies.name: Hyper Metabolism
technologies.text: During the status phase, gain 3 command tokens instead of 2

technologies.name: Indoctrination Team
technologies.text: During the agenda phase, after an outcome you voted for is resolved, place 2 infantry from your reinforcements on a planet you control.

technologies.name: Instinct Training
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card and spend 1 token from your strategy pool when another player plays an action card; cancel that action card

technologies.name: Mageon Implants
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to look at another player's hand of action cards. Choose 1 of those cards and add it to your hand

technologies.name: Networked Command
technologies.text: Up to 3 ships in your home system do not count against your fleet pool. When you cast 1 or more votes, you may exhaust this card to cast an additional number of votes equal to the number of command tokens in your fleet pool.

technologies.name: Neural Motivator
technologies.text: During the status phase, draw 2 action cards instead of 1.

technologies.name: Neuroglaive
technologies.text: After another player activates a system that contains 1 or more of your ships, that player removes 1 token from their fleet pool and returns it to their reinforcements

technologies.name: Pre-Fab Arcologies
technologies.text: After you explore a planet, ready that planet

technologies.name: Production Biomes
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card and spend 1 token from your strategy pool to gain 4 trade goods and choose 1 other player; that player gains 2 trade goods

technologies.name: Psychoactive Armaments
technologies.text: After your opponent makes a combat roll, you may exhaust this card. If you do, for each of their units that did not produce a hit, you may reroll that unit's combat roll; any hits that roll produces are produced against your opponent's units instead.

technologies.name: Psychoarchaeology
technologies.text: You can use technology specialties on planets you control without exhausting them, even if those planets are exhausted During the Action Phase, you can exhaust planets you control that have technology specialties to gain 1 Trade Good

technologies.name: Psychographics
technologies.text: During the status phase, you may score 1 additional public objective instead of a secret objective.

technologies.name: Stone's Embrace
technologies.text: When you would spend a token from your strategy pool, you may exhaust 1 planet you control to spend 1 token from your reinforcements instead.

technologies.name: Tempest Drive
technologies.text: After you win a combat in a system, you may exhaust this card to remove 1 command token from that system.

technologies.name: Transparasteel Plating
technologies.text: During your turn of the action phase, players that have passed cannot play action cards

technologies.name: Unity Algorithm
technologies.text: Once per agenda phase, after an agenda is revealed, you may predict aloud an outcome of that agenda. If your prediction is correct, you may score 1 public objective if you fulfill its requirements; each other player who voted for that outcome draws 1 secret objective.

technologies.name: Voidwatch
technologies.text: After a player moves ships into a system that contains 1 or more of your units, they must give you 1 promissory note from their hand, if able

technologies.name: War Song Implants
technologies.text: After you win a space combat, you may ready 1 planet you control.

technologies.name: X-89 Bacterial Weapon
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card and choose 1 planet in a system that contains 1 or more of your ships that have BOMBARDMENT; destroy all infantry on that planet

technologies.name: X-89 Bacterial Weapon Ω
technologies.text: After 1 or more of your units use BOMBARDMENT against a planet, if at least 1 of your opponent's infantry was destroyed, you may destroy all of your opponent's infantry on that planet.

technologies.name: X-89 Bacterial Weapon ΩΩ
technologies.text: Double the hits produced by your units' BOMBARDMENT and ground combat rolls. Exhaust each planet you use BOMBARDMENT against.

technologies.name: Yin Spinner
technologies.text: After 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION, place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on a planet you control in that system

technologies.name: Yin Spinner Ω
technologies.text: Ω: After you produce units, place up to 2 infantry from your reinforcements on any planet you control or in any space area that contains 1 or more of your ships

technologies.name: Zhrgar Stimulants
technologies.text: Once per action, after a player produces 1 or more hits during a round of combat in a system that contains a Glory token, gain 1 trade good. After a player rolls combat dice, you may exhaust this card to allow that player to reroll any number of those dice.

technologies.name: Aerie Hololattice
technologies.text: Other players cannot move ships through systems that contain your structures. Each planet that contains 1 or more of your structures gains the PRODUCTION 1 ability as if it were a unit

technologies.name: Agency Supply Network
technologies.text: Whenever you resolve one of your PRODUCTION abilities, you may resolve an additional one of your PRODUCTION abilities in any system; the additional use does not trigger this ability.

technologies.name: Contractual Obligations
technologies.text: At the start of the agenda phase, for each unit upgrade technology you control, you may choose 1 player. Each of those players must produce 1 ship in a system that contains 1 or more of their space docks or war suns.

technologies.name: Covert Strike Teams
technologies.text: At the start of a ground combat, you may roll 1 die for each of up to 2 of your ground forces on that planet. For each result equal to or greater than that unit's combat value, produce 1 hit; your opponent must assign it to 1 of their units on that planet.

technologies.name: Daedalon Flight System
technologies.text: After a round of space combat in a system that contains 1 or more of your mechs, you may have 1 of those units become damaged to produce 1 hit and assign it to a cruiser, dreadnought, or destroyer you do not control in that system.

technologies.name: E-Res Siphons
technologies.text: After another player activates a system that contains 1 or more of your ships, gain 4 trade goods

technologies.name: Emergency Deployment
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to place or move 1 of your space docks onto a planet you control.

technologies.name: Encrypted Trade Hub
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card to allow a player to exchange 1 of their relics or agendas as part of a transaction. You always vote last during the agenda phase. When an outcome you voted for or predicted is resolved, each player who voted for that outcome gains 1 commodity.

technologies.name: Fabrication Grid
technologies.text: Each system that contains 1 or more of your ships and no planets gains PRODUCTION 2 as if it were a unit you control.

technologies.name: Graviton Laser System
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card before 1 or more of your units uses Space Cannon; hits produced by those units must be assigned to non-fighter ships if able

technologies.name: Hegemonic Trade Policy
technologies.text: Exhaust this card when 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION; swap the resource and influence values of 1 planet you control during that use of Production

technologies.name: I.I.H.Q. Modernization
technologies.text: (Moved to Breakthrough in Thunder's Edge Expansion) You are neighbors with all players that have units or control planets in or adjacent to the Mecatol Rex system. Gain the Custodia Vigilia planet card and its legendary planet ability card. You cannot lose these cards, and this card cannot have an X or Y assimilator token placed on it.

technologies.name: Impressment Programs
technologies.text: When you explore a planet, you may produce 1 ship in that planet's system. ACTION: Exhaust this card and 1 planet you control to explore that planet.

technologies.name: Impulse Core
technologies.text: At the start of a space combat, you may destroy 1 of your cruisers or destroyers in the active system to produce 1 hit against your opponent's ships; that hit must be assigned by your opponent to 1 of their non-fighters ships if able

technologies.name: Inheritance Systems
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card and spend 2 resources when you research a technology; ignore all of that technology's prerequisites

technologies.name: Integrated Economy
technologies.text: After you gain control of a planet, you may produce any number of units on that planet that have a combined cost equal to or less than that planet's resource value.

technologies.name: Krovoz Strike Teams
technologies.text: After another player's unit uses SUSTAIN DAMAGE to cancel a hit produced by your units or abilities, you may exhaust this card to destroy that unit. After you produce 1 or more hits during a round of combat, you may spend 1 trade good to produce 1 additional hit.

technologies.name: L4 Disruptors
technologies.text: During an invasion, units cannot use SPACE CANNON against your units

technologies.name: Local Contracts
technologies.text: During the action phase: You may exhaust this card to use the PRODUCTION ability of 1 of your units. Then, place 1 command token from your reinforcements in that unit's system.

technologies.name: Merged Replicators
technologies.text: When 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION, you may exhaust this card to swap 1 of those unit's PRODUCTION with another on the game board, or apply +2 to the total PRODUCTION value of those units.

technologies.name: Mirror Computing
technologies.text: When you spend trade goods, each trade good is worth 2 resources or influence instead of 1

technologies.name: Nullification Field
technologies.text: After another player activates a system that contains 1 or more of your ships, you may exhaust this card and spend 1 token from your strategy pool; immediately end that player's turn

technologies.name: Parallel Production
technologies.text: When you produce 1 or more units, you may produce 1 additional unit in each of 2 systems that contain 1 or more of your ships and 1 of your command tokens.

technologies.name: Predictive Intelligence
technologies.text: At the end of your turn, you may exhaust this card to redistribute your command tokens When you cast votes during the agenda phase, you may cast 3 additional votes; if you do, and the outcome you voted for is not resolved, exhaust this card

technologies.name: Quantum Datahub Node
technologies.text: At the end of the strategy phase, you may spend 1 token from your strategy pool and give another player 3 of your trade goods. If you do, give 1 of your strategy cards to that player and take 1 of their strategy cards

technologies.name: Recursive Worm
technologies.text: At the start of your turn, you may exhaust this card to choose 1 non-home system other than Mecatol Rex that contains 1 or more of your units; place 1 other player's command token from their reinforcements in that system.

technologies.name: Salvage Operations
technologies.text: After you win or lose a space combat, gain 1 trade good; if you won the combat, you may also produce 1 ship in that system of any ship type that was destroyed during the combat

technologies.name: Sanctification Field
technologies.text: When you activate a system, or a system that contains 1 or more of your units is activated, you may choose 1 technology or non-fighter, non-structure unit upgrade technology another player controls; until the end of this tactical action, that card is treated as though it had no ability text.

technologies.name: Sarween Tools
technologies.text: When 1 or more of your units use Production, reduce the combined cost of the produced units by 1

technologies.name: Scanlink Drone Network
technologies.text: When you activate a system, you may explore 1 planet in that system which contains 1 or more of your units

technologies.name: Seeker Drones
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to choose 1 of your neighbors that has 1 or more relic fragments. That player gains 2 trade goods and must give you 1 of those relic fragments of your choice

technologies.name: Sentient Datapool
technologies.text: At the start of the status phase, you may spend 3 trade goods to research 1 technology.

technologies.name: Temporal Command Suite
technologies.text: After any player's agent becomes exhausted, you may exhaust this card to ready that agent; if you ready another player's agent you may perform a transaction with that player

technologies.name: Transit Diodes
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card at the start of your turn during the action phase; remove up to 4 of your ground forces from the game board and place them on 1 or more planets you control.

technologies.name: Vector Program
technologies.text: At the start of the strategy phase, you may place any number of your trade goods on any combination of strategy cards. Then, place 2 trade goods from the supply on 1 strategy card

technologies.name: Voidwake Missiles
technologies.text: After 1 or more of your units with SUSTAIN DAMAGE makes a combat roll, you may choose 1 of those units to become damaged to reroll its combat roll.

technologies.name: Aetherstream
technologies.text: After you or one of your neighbors activates a system that is adjacent to an anomaly, you may apply +1 to the move value of all of that player's ships during this tactical action

technologies.name: Antimass Deflectors
technologies.text: Your ships can move into and through asteroid fields When other players' units use SPACE CANNON against your units, apply -1 to the result of each die roll

technologies.name: Chaos Mapping
technologies.text: Other players cannot activate asteroid fields that contain 1 or more of your ships. At the start of your turn during the action phase, you may produce 1 unit in a system that contains at least 1 of your units that has Production

technologies.name: Dark Energy Tap
technologies.text: After you perform a tactical action in a system that contains a frontier token, if you have 1 or more ships in that system, explore that token Your ships can retreat into adjacent systems that do not contain other players' units, even if you do not have units or control planets in that system.

technologies.name: Fleet Logistics
technologies.text: During each of your turns of the action phase, you may perform 2 actions instead of 1

technologies.name: Geosympathic Impeller
technologies.text: After you activate a system, you may exhaust a planet you control of the same type as a planet in that system to apply +1 to the move value of each of your ships during this tactical action.

technologies.name: Gravity Drive
technologies.text: After you activate a system, apply +1 to the move value of 1 of your ships during this tactical action

technologies.name: Lazax Gate Folding
technologies.text: During your tactical actions, if you do not control Mecatol Rex, treat its system as if it contains both an alpha and beta wormhole. ACTION: If you control Mecatol Rex, exhaust this card to place 1 infantry from your reinforcements on Mecatol Rex

technologies.name: Light/Wave Deflector
technologies.text: Your ships can move through systems that contain other players' ships

technologies.name: Lightning Drives
technologies.text: During movement, you may increase the move value of each of your non-fighter ships not transporting fighters or infantry by 1.

technologies.name: Midas Turbine
technologies.text: After you activate a system, you may exhaust this card and spend any number of trade goods; for each trade good you spent, apply +1 to the move value of 1 non-fighter ship you control.

technologies.name: Pilgrimage Beacons
technologies.text: Once during your turn, when you exhaust 1 or more planets you control that have a technology specialty, you may produce 1 ship in 1 system that contains 1 of those planets.

technologies.name: Rift Engines
technologies.text: After you activate a system, you may exhaust this card to choose 1 ship you control and roll a die. On a result of 1-3, remove that ship from the game board; on a result of 4-10, apply +2 to that ship's move value during this tactical action.

technologies.name: Shrouded Skirmishers
technologies.text: At the start of a space combat, you may choose 1 of your ships in the active system that did not begin this action in that system; roll a die. On a result equal to or greater than that ship's combat value, produce 1 hit; your opponent must assign it to 1 of their ships.

technologies.name: Sling Relay
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to produce 1 ship in any system that contains 1 of your space docks

technologies.name: Spacial Conduit Cylinder
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card after you activate a system that contains 1 or more of your units; that system is adjacent to all other systems that contain 1 or more of your units during this activation

technologies.name: Spark Thrusters
technologies.text: ACTION: Spend 1 command token from your strategy pool or purge 1 relic fragment to move 1 of your ships to an adjacent system that contains no other player's ships. If you spent a command token, exhaust this card.

technologies.name: Wormhole Generator
technologies.text: At the start of the status phase, place or move a Creuss wormhole token into either a system that contains a planet you control or a non-home system that does not contain another player's ships

technologies.name: Wormhole Generator Ω
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to place or move a Creuss wormhole token into either a system that contains a planet you control or a non-home system that does not contain another player's ships

technologies.name: Wraith Engine
technologies.text: After you activate a non-home system, you may exhaust this card to choose 1 ship you control; you may treat that ship as adjacent to the active system until the end of this tactical action.

technologies.name: Advanced Carrier II
technologies.text: Advanced Carrier II is a carrier unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 3, combat 9, move 2, capacity 8. Abilities: Sustain Damage. Sustain Damage.

technologies.name: Aegis I
technologies.text: Aegis I is a dreadnought unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 4.

technologies.name: Blockade Runner I
technologies.text: Blockade Runner I is a destroyer unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 1.

technologies.name: Carrier II
technologies.text: Carrier II is a carrier unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 3, combat 9, move 2, capacity 6.

technologies.name: Chitin Hulk I
technologies.text: Chitin Hulk I is a dreadnought unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 4.

technologies.name: Combat Transport I
technologies.text: Combat Transport I is a carrier unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 3.

technologies.name: Corsair I
technologies.text: Corsair I is a fighter unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 1 (x2).

technologies.name: Crimson Legionnaire II
technologies.text: Crimson Legionnaire II is a infantry unit upgrade. Attributes: combat 7. After this unit is destroyed, gain 1 commodity or convert 1 of your commodities to a trade good. Then, place the unit on this card. At the start of your next turn, place each unit that is on this card on a planet you control in your home system.

technologies.name: Cruiser II
technologies.text: Cruiser II is a cruiser unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 2, combat 6, move 3, capacity 1.

technologies.name: Destroyer II
technologies.text: Destroyer II is a destroyer unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 1, combat 8, move 2. Abilities: Anti-Fighter Barrage. Anti-Fighter Barrage 6 (x3).

technologies.name: Dimensional Tear II
technologies.text: Dimensional Tear II is a space dock unit upgrade. This system is a gravity rift; your ships do not roll for this gravity rift.

technologies.name: Dreadnought II
technologies.text: Dreadnought II is a dreadnought unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 4, combat 5, move 2, capacity 1. This unit cannot be destroyed by "Direct Hit" action cards.

technologies.name: Exotrireme II
technologies.text: Exotrireme II is a dreadnought unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 4, combat 5, move 2, capacity 1. This unit cannot be destroyed by "Direct Hit" action cards.

technologies.name: Fighter II
technologies.text: Fighter II is a fighter unit upgrade. Attributes: combat 8, move 2. This unit may move without being transported.

technologies.name: Floating Factory II
technologies.text: Floating Factory II is a space dock unit upgrade. Attributes: move 2, capacity 5. This unit is placed in the space area instead of on a planet.

technologies.name: Gauss Cannon I
technologies.text: Gauss Cannon I is a pds unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). -.

technologies.name: Heavy Bomber I
technologies.text: Heavy Bomber I is a fighter unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 1 (x2).

technologies.name: Hel Titan II
technologies.text: Hel Titan II is a pds unit upgrade. Attributes: combat 6. This unit is treated as both a structure and a ground force. It cannot be transported.

technologies.name: Hybrid Crystal Fighter II
technologies.text: Hybrid Crystal Fighter II is a fighter unit upgrade. Attributes: combat 7, move 2. This unit may move without being transported.

technologies.name: Impactor I
technologies.text: Impactor I is a infantry unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 1 (x2).

technologies.name: Infantry II
technologies.text: Infantry II is a infantry unit upgrade. Attributes: combat 7. After this unit is destroyed, roll 1 die. If the result is 6 or greater, place the unit on this card. At the start of your next turn, place each unit that is on this card on a planet you control in your home system.

technologies.name: Lancer Dreadnought I
technologies.text: Lancer Dreadnought I is a dreadnought unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 4.

technologies.name: Letani Warrior II
technologies.text: Letani Warrior II is a infantry unit upgrade. Attributes: combat 7. After this unit is destroyed, roll 1 die. If the result is 6 or greater, place the unit on this card. At the start of your next turn, place each unit that is on this card on a planet you control in your home system.

technologies.name: Level 0
technologies.text: Level 0 is a unknown unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). Level 1.

technologies.name: Memoria II
technologies.text: Memoria II is a flagship unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 8, move 2, capacity 6.

technologies.name: Mycelium Ring I
technologies.text: Mycelium Ring I is a space dock unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). Planetary Shield.

technologies.name: Orion Platform I
technologies.text: Orion Platform I is a pds unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). Planetary Shield.

technologies.name: PDS II
technologies.text: PDS II is a pds unit upgrade. You may use this unit's SPACE CANNON against ships that are adjacent to this unit's system.

technologies.name: Prototype War Sun II
technologies.text: Prototype War Sun II is a war sun unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 10, move 3, capacity 6. Abilities: Planetary Shield.

technologies.name: Raider I
technologies.text: Raider I is a cruiser unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 2.

technologies.name: Sabre I
technologies.text: Sabre I is a destroyer unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 1.

technologies.name: Saturn Engine II
technologies.text: Saturn Engine II is a cruiser unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 2, combat 6, move 3, capacity 2. Abilities: Sustain Damage. Sustain Damage.

technologies.name: Shattered Sky I
technologies.text: Shattered Sky I is a cruiser unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 2.

technologies.name: Space Dock II
technologies.text: Space Dock II is a space dock unit upgrade. This unit's PRODUCTION value is equal to 4 more than the resource value of this planet.

technologies.name: Spec Ops II
technologies.text: Spec Ops II is a infantry unit upgrade. Attributes: combat 6. After this unit is destroyed, roll 1 die. If the result is 5 or greater, place the unit on this card. At the start of your next turn, place each unit that is on this card on a planet you control in your home system.

technologies.name: Star Dragon I
technologies.text: Star Dragon I is a carrier unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 3.

technologies.name: Strike Wing Alpha II
technologies.text: Strike Wing Alpha II is a destroyer unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 1, combat 7, move 2, capacity 1. Abilities: Anti-Fighter Barrage. When this unit uses ANTI-FIGHTER BARRAGE, each result of 9 or 10 also destroys 1 of your opponents infantry in the space area of the active system.

technologies.name: Super-Dreadnought II
technologies.text: Super-Dreadnought II is a dreadnought unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 4, combat 4, move 2, capacity 2. This unit cannot be destroyed by "Direct Hit" action cards.

technologies.name: Terrafactory I
technologies.text: Terrafactory I is a war sun unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). -.

technologies.name: Trade Port I
technologies.text: Trade Port I is a space dock unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). Anti-Fighter Barrage 6 (x2).

technologies.name: Unholy Abomination I
technologies.text: Unholy Abomination I is a infantry unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 1.

technologies.name: Voidflare Warden I
technologies.text: Voidflare Warden I is a mech unit upgrade (Discordant Stars). 2.

technologies.name: War Sun
technologies.text: War Sun is a war sun unit upgrade. Attributes: cost 12, move 2, capacity 6. Abilities: Planetary Shield.

technologies.name: AI Development Algorithm
technologies.text: When you research a unit upgrade technology, you may exhaust this card to ignore any 1 prerequisite When 1 or more of your units use Production, you may exhaust this card to reduce the combined cost of the produced units by the number of unit upgrade technologies that you own

technologies.name: ATS Armaments
technologies.text: After you explore, or purge 1 or more relic fragments, place 1 commodity token on this card. At the start of a round of combat, remove any number of tokens from this card to reroll up to that many of your dice during this round of combat.

technologies.name: Assault Cannon
technologies.text: At the start of a space combat in a system that contains 3 or more of your non-fighter ships, your opponent must destroy 1 of their non-fighter ships

technologies.name: Brood Pod
technologies.text: After another player activates a system that contains 1 of your structures, you may exhaust this card to produce 1 ship in the active system. For each of your structures in the active system, you may remove that unit from the game board to reduce the cost of the produced ship by 3.

technologies.name: Deliverance Engine
technologies.text: Once per space combat, after 1 of your non-fighter ships is destroyed, you may produce 1 hit and assign it to 1 of your opponent's non-carrier ships.

technologies.name: Dimensional Splicer
technologies.text: At the start of space combat in a system that contains a wormhole and 1 or more of your ships, you may produce 1 hit and assign it to 1 of your opponent's ships

technologies.name: Duranium Armor
technologies.text: During each combat round, after you assign hits to your units, repair 1 of your damaged units that did not use Sustain Damage during this combat round.

technologies.name: Emergency Mobilization Protocols
technologies.text: At the end of your turn, you may exhaust this card and spend 1 token from your strategy pool to remove a command token from a system that contains 1 or more of your space docks.

technologies.name: False Flag Operations
technologies.text: At the start of your turn, you may exhaust this card and 1 planet you control to ready 1 non-home planet other than Mecatol Rex.

technologies.name: Fractal Plating
technologies.text: After a unit you control in a system is destroyed, you may exhaust this card to place 1 unit of that type from your reinforcements in the space area of a system adjacent to that system that contains 1 or more of your ships.

technologies.name: Magen Defense Grid
technologies.text: You may exhaust this card at the start of a round of ground combat on a planet that contains 1 or more of your units that have Planetary Shield; your opponent cannot make combat rolls this combat round.

technologies.name: Magen Defense Grid Ω
technologies.text: At the start of ground combat on a planet that contains 1 or more of your structures, you may produce 1 hit and assign it to 1 of your opponent's ground forces.

technologies.name: Magen Defense Grid ΩΩ
technologies.text: When any player activates a system that contains 1 or more of your structures, place 1 infantry from your reinforcements with each of those structures. At the start of ground combat on a planet that contains 1 or more of your structures, produce 1 hit and assign it to 1 of your opponent's ground forces.

technologies.name: Magmus Reactor
technologies.text: Your ships can move into supernovas. After 1 or more of your units use Production in a system that either contains a war sun or is adjacent to a supernova, gain 1 trade good

technologies.name: Magmus Reactor Ω
technologies.text: Your ships can move into supernovas. Each supernova that contains 1 or more of your units gains the PRODUCTION 5 ability as if it were 1 of your units

technologies.name: Non-Euclidean Shielding
technologies.text: When 1 of your units uses SUSTAIN DAMAGE, cancel 2 hits instead of 1

technologies.name: Omniscience Field
technologies.text: After a system is activated, you may exhaust this card to choose 1 player. Apply +1 or -1 to all rolls that player makes during this tactical action.

technologies.name: Orbital Defense Grid
technologies.text: At the start of invasion, choose up to 1 unit you control in the active system; that unit gains PLANETARY SHIELD. When a player commits a unit to land on a planet you control, you may exhaust this card to destroy that unit.

technologies.name: Plasma Scoring
technologies.text: When 1 or more of your units use Bombardment or Space Cannon, 1 of those units may roll 1 additional die

technologies.name: SEIDR Project
technologies.text: At the end of each round of space combat, you may use the SPACE CANNON of 1 of your units in the active system against your opponent's ships, without rolling additional dice.

technologies.name: Scavenger Exos
technologies.text: Once per action, after you win a ground combat, you may draw 1 action card.

technologies.name: Self Assembly Routines
technologies.text: After 1 or more of your units use PRODUCTION, you may exhaust this card to place 1 mech from your reinforcements on a planet you control in that system After 1 of your mechs is destroyed, gain 1 trade good

technologies.name: Shard Volley
technologies.text: When 1 or more of your units produce 1 or more hits during a BOMBARDMENT roll against a planet, produce 1 additional hit for that roll.

technologies.name: Supercharge
technologies.text: At the start of a combat round, you may exhaust this card to apply +1 to the result of each of your unit's combat rolls during this combat round

technologies.name: Valkyrie Particle Weave
technologies.text: After making combat rolls during a round of ground combat, if your opponent produced 1 or more hits, you produce 1 additional hit

technologies.name: Vortex
technologies.text: ACTION: Exhaust this card to choose another player's non-structure unit in a system that is adjacent to 1 or more of your space docks. Capture 1 unit of that type from that player's reinforcements
